generalInfo: Hokkien is a Southern Min language continuum with shared roots and rich regional variation. This learning center helps compare dialects while preserving local forms through community contributions.

dialects.name: Quanzhou (泉州)
dialects.notes: One of the historical core varieties with conservative features in some phonological areas.

dialects.name: Zhangzhou (漳州)
dialects.notes: Another major historical base variety, influential in Taiwan and Southeast Asian communities.

dialects.name: Xiamen / Amoy (廈門)
dialects.notes: Often treated as a prestige urban variety and important in educational and reference materials.

dialects.name: Taiwanese
dialects.notes: A broad local standard shaped historically by Quanzhou/Zhangzhou contact and modern media usage.

dialects.name: Southeast Asian Hokkien (General)
dialects.notes: Umbrella for diaspora varieties influenced by Malay, English, Tagalog, Indonesian, Thai, and Burmese contexts.

dialects.name: Malaysian Hokkien (North)
dialects.notes: Commonly associated with Penang and northern Peninsula speech communities.

dialects.name: Malaysian Hokkien (Central)
dialects.notes: Central region mix with contact influence from Mandarin, Cantonese, Malay, and English.

dialects.name: Malaysian Hokkien (South)
dialects.notes: Southern region communities with strong multilingual contact effects.

dialects.name: Singaporean Hokkien
dialects.notes: Multilingual contact variety with English and Malay influence.

dialects.name: Philippine Hokkien
dialects.notes: Often includes lexical contact with Filipino and English, varying by city/community.

dialects.name: Indonesian Hokkien
dialects.notes: Multiple local forms with Indonesian/Malay and Chinese language contact patterns.

dialects.name: Burmese Hokkien
dialects.notes: Community heritage variety with local phonetic and lexical adaptation.

dialects.name: Thai Hokkien
dialects.notes: Heritage diaspora variety with Thai contact effects, often preserving older forms in some families.

dialects.name: Shared / Cross-dialect
dialects.notes: Entries useful across multiple Hokkien communities.

MOE Taiwanese Hokkien Dictionary (官方台語辭典)

pronunciationTips: Track tone changes (tone sandhi) in connected speech.
Check both POJ and Tâi-lô spellings when studying.
Collect local variants with notes instead of forcing one form.
